title: ctenitopsis matthewii
intro: Ctenitopsis matthewii, also known as Ctenitis matthewii, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the subtropical and tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory compounds. Traditionally, the leaves of Ctenitopsis matthewii are prepared into tea or infusions for its herbal benefits, which include reducing pain, improving circulation, and acting as an antioxidant.
